videos.title: Number Patterns | Finding the Rule in Number Sequences
videos.description: Learn how to find the rule in number patterns. Practice with sequences that add, subtract, or multiply to find what comes next.
learningObjectives: Find the rule in a number sequence
Predict the next number using the rule
keyConceptsCovered: Looking at differences between consecutive numbers

videos.title: Skip Counting Patterns | Number Sequences with Addition
videos.description: Explore number sequences that use addition. Count by 3s, 4s, 6s, and 7s to create number patterns. Find the rule and extend each sequence.
learningObjectives: Create number sequences by skip counting with various intervals
Identify the addition rule in a number sequence
keyConceptsCovered: All addition sequences have a constant difference

videos.title: Number Patterns in Tables and Charts | Organizing Sequences
videos.description: Learn to organize number sequences in tables. Use input-output tables to discover the rule. See how tables make it easier to spot and extend patterns.
learningObjectives: Use tables to organize and find number patterns
Complete input-output tables using the pattern rule
keyConceptsCovered: Tables make it easy to check if a rule works

videos.title: Find the Missing Number | Number Sequence Practice
videos.description: Practice finding missing numbers in sequences. Each puzzle gives you a sequence with a blank, and you need to figure out the rule to fill in the gap.
learningObjectives: Find missing numbers in sequences by applying the rule
Verify answers by checking if the rule holds for all numbers
keyConceptsCovered: Finding the rule from given numbers
Filling in blanks at any position
Checking answers by testing the rule

videos.title: Tricky Number Patterns | Two-Step Rules and Backwards Sequences
videos.description: Ready for a challenge? Try number patterns with two-step rules (like add 2 then add 3) and backwards sequences (counting down). Can you crack the code?
learningObjectives: Solve number patterns with non-constant differences
keyConceptsCovered: Backwards sequences: subtract to find the pattern
Some patterns change the amount they grow by

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Fast-track with tables, practice, and tricky pattern challenge

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of number sequences with practice and challenge

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Core number sequence concepts after prerequisite review

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: What comes next in the pattern: 4, 8, 12, 16, __?
questions.explanation: The rule is add 4 each time. 16 + 4 = 20. This is a skip counting pattern by 4s.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Use a hundreds chart to help students see number patterns visually. Highlight skip counting sequences with different colors. Videos 1-3 are essential for all students.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Practice number sequences at home: count steps while climbing stairs by 2s, 3s, or 5s. Play 'What comes next?' with number cards.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Look at the difference between numbers in a sequence. If the difference is always the same, you have found the rule! Try making your own number patterns.
